metadata.description: P4 — the prompty / prompter / pioneer / puppeteer meta-prompting framework; ships the Roboto four-lens reasoning instance and the standalone epistemic plugins (vlds, verification-discipline) as installable plugins.

plugins.name: mister
plugins.description: Roboto / "The Init Elegance" — the four-lens reasoning instance of the P4 framework: identity, rubric, vlds, templates, bias-patterns, isomorphic-operations, sjc-indexer, activation, and persistence skills plus a roboto subagent.

plugins.name: vlds
plugins.description: VLDS — the P4 framework's epistemic discipline as a standalone, runtime dashboard of three instruments plus a conductor. The gate routes every load-bearing claim through VERIFIABLE → VERIFIED → CONFIRMED / PENDING / HEDGED — verify before you assert, state what you can't verify with its uncertainty attached. The guide loop disciplines the need behind the claim, configuring and recording the model's behavior as the user steers it. The inspector re-examines a high-stakes verdict through independent perspectives to corroborate, overturn, or surface it — the outside eye the others cannot be. The instruments are invoked directly; a fourth skill, the looper, runs them in order and logs each decision to a shared, user-editable log. Portable; no dependency on the roboto instance.

plugins.name: verification-discipline
plugins.description: Verification Discipline (truth-on-a-timer) — a standalone, self-obeying framework for WHEN a stale-or-wrong prior must be re-checked before it drives an action. Half-life decay classes decide when a prior has expired; an independent cost gate fires when a cheap canonical oracle exists for a load-bearing claim; confidence is barred as an input; and an action-scoped layer-priority rule keeps a stale policy from suppressing the check (verification is never insubordination). Sixteen rules in three groups, three mottos, and the live failure case they were derived from. Companion to the vlds gate — the when-to-verify policy behind it, not a second gate. Standalone; no external plugin dependencies.
